creator: se@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Gerald Pfeifer from comment #3)

Transparent support for i386 binaries on amd64 exists in FreeBSD since May 2003 and I'm not aware of any open issues - but that is irrelevant for this PR.


The problem reported in this PR is due to the version check performed by the Wine application when the 64 bit version is started in a mode that is able to execute 32 Windows binaries.

In that case, a matching (identical version) package of Wine for i386 is automatically fetched - but does not exist immediately after an update of the port. It takes a few days for the package builders to catch up with a port update, and during that time the automatic fetching of the i386 version fails.

In the PR the amd64 version 6.0.3 of wine has been installed, while the latest official i386 package available on the mirrors is still at version 6.0.2.

I do not know of an easy way to build the wine ports for i386 on amd64 (there used to be a separate port, but now there is a single port that detects the architecture of the build host and then builds either the amd64 or i386 version.

Locally building the i386 version on amd64 is possible with poudriere in an i386 jail.

Or delay the update of wine on amd64 until the new version of the "official" i386 package is available for installation.
